fun fact about thais and the bird twins actually! thais is based off shub-niggurath, while the birds twins are based off of zhar and lloigor; in the Cthulhu mythos, zhar and lloigor are the sons of shub-niggurath 

which isn't necessarily indicative of anything, but I think it's neat (and might by why the bird twins were put in the desert/got some interactions with thais over any other awakener, perhaps)

Here we go 

Chapter 7

|| Keeper, Ramona, Ryker, and William go on a very important mission to Remoahue (butchered spelling) to retrieve or destroy the mother tree as reports found of it causing dissolution on a continental to maybe global scale. They travel to the dessert and encounter Nubia, they aren't sure what they were but kill them. 

Eventually discovering Juliette and the Aramites. Juliette proclaims to be the foretold savior of the Aram that can brace the storm wall of course later we find out this is false.

The Aram practice a ritualistic thing to continue their numbers in crude terms an orgy, they make babies quickly and the children grow quickly. But this is the fate and curse of the Aram.

As we investigate the Aram, tree and Juliette. Herbert sneaks up with Ghelot(explained more in special op) and unfortunately kills our beloved Senior William who smells of Cedar

We meet Thais, she's the queen of the Aram but due to dissolution her presence was down to a ghost where only keeper could see her. The big twist was that nobody else could see her. 

Juliette pulls the trigger once they reached the Mother Tree and corrupts it, basically triggering the boss battle. The Aram go into the mother tree still believing Juliette to be the savior. Ramona isn't feeling well due to the Three Aeons power repelling each other, the fight with the mother tree is harsh that Tawil had to step in to help. As we defeat and fall Thais transfer the last seed of the mother tree into our body via a kiss..this will matter later. ||
The matriach is is a royal title/setting whilst mpreg is just the switching of who carries a child
